title: eriogonum gracile var. incultum
intro: Eriogonum gracile var. incultum, also known as Sulphur-flower Buckwheat, is a perennial plant species in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum gracile var. incultum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
